name: Independent Code Review
description: Changes are reviewed by an independent code reviewer prior to code being merged and released to production. Independent code review restricts the ability for anyone individual from taking a change production without involving an independent party (4-eyes principle).

attributes.text: Code Reviews are performed prior to committing a change to the main branch
testable_criteria: For each sampled change merged to the main branch, evidence shows a code review occurred before the merge to the main branch.
For each sampled change merged to the main branch, the code review record is associated with the specific change being merged.

attributes.text: Code Review approvals are performed by independent code reviewers
testable_criteria: For each sampled change, the person who approved the code review is not the same person who authored the change.
For each sampled change, at least one approval from an independent reviewer exists before the change is merged to the main branch.

attributes.text: Testing is performed in accordance with the testing policy
testable_criteria: Tests run in the CI/CD pipeline before merge.
Test results are visible in pull request status checks.
All unit tests pass with zero failures before release.
All integration tests pass before release.
Critical user journeys pass end-to-end tests before release.
IF performance testing is applicable, performance benchmarks are met before release.
Security scan results are reviewed and approved before release.
Tests pass in a clean environment with no cached dependencies.
Flaky tests are fixed or removed before release.
All automated tests pass before release.
Test coverage meets the minimum thresholds before release.
No known critical or high-severity bugs exist before release.
IF performance testing is applicable, performance tests pass before release.
Security tests pass before release.
Manual smoke tests are completed for critical features before release.
For all new code, unit test line coverage is at least 80%.
For critical integration points, integration test coverage is at least 60%.
IF the change touches security-sensitive, payment, or authentication flows, coverage is 100% for those critical paths.
For legacy code, existing coverage is maintained or improved.
Branch coverage is at least 70%.
Function coverage is at least 80%.
Coverage reports are generated as part of the code review process.
Coverage reports are reviewed as part of the code review process.
Tests are deterministic and repeatable.
Tests are independent and do not rely on execution order.
Tests clean up after themselves and leave no side effects.
Test names clearly describe what is being tested.
Tests include both positive and negative test cases.
Tests that fail are fixed before code merge.
IF an exception is claimed, the change fits one of the allowed exception categories in the testing policy.
